assessment: Imagine you are a new Accounts Assistant at a growing company. As part of your role, you need
to ensure that all financial transactions are recorded in accordance with a clear understanding of
the Books of Prime Entry.
Your Finance Manager has tasked you to identify and explain the Books of Prime Entry that
the company must use to maintain accurate and organised records. The company’s transactions
include cash and credit sales, cash and credit purchases among others.

lessons.title: Identify the Books of Prime Entry that the Company should use for the following types
topics: of transactions:
●Credit Sales
●Cash Sales
●Credit Purchases
●Cash Purchases

activities: Stating the Source Document for each Book of Prime Entry
CONTENT TIPS
Source Documents
Source Documents are written or printed records, which provide proof of a Business Transaction.
Books of Prime Entry are prepared using source documents. They provide details for which
Books of Prime Entry are to be prepared. The design of each document differs from one Business
to the other, but all of them will contain similar information.
Importance of Source Documents
assessment: DMB a Small Retailer of various goods, has challenges in identifying the Source Document to
use in recording Business Transactions in the Books of Prime Entry. The firm has requested you
as a Business and Finance Learner, to help the them identify the Source Document to be used to
prepare each of the followings Books of Prime Entry
i. Purchases Day Book
ii. Cash Book (Bank Column only)
